Name: CTA bar
Description: Contains the navigational and action buttons. Action buttons, such as Save, Close, Delete, are on the right. Place the primary button—the one that guides the user toward the default action on the far right.

Name: Add value link
Default State: The text field is in the focus state, so that lets users enter text directly.
Description: The add link lets users add a new value to the list.
Dynamic Behavior:
On clicking the ‘add’ link, system displays input box inline with a label and helper text. The cursor shows in the input box directly. Click check mark to complete the task. Click cross button to cancel the task. The add link button disappears from the top until the inline input box is present.

Add value:
User can add a value A and select and apply it, this value A will be in the list. 

Remove value:
When the user visits the next time and they deselect this value A, then this custom value A won't be there.
Error State:
If no object is added and the user press check mark, the system shows an error state in the inline edit label.

”Enter values to continue”

Name: Delete caution alert message

Name: Dropdown menu
Description: This dropdown menu display sort and filter option
Dynamic Behavior:
Clicking on the column header will display this dropdown menu.

If the column only have filter function, clicking on the column header will open the filter modal dialog box directly.

Name: Batch actions
Description:
Batch actions are functions that are performed on multiple items within a table.

Actions include but not limited to: Favourite, Share, Edit, Export, Duplicate, Delete etc.
Dynamic Behavior:
Once the user selects at least one row from the table, the batch action bar appears at the top of the table, presenting the user with actions they can take. 

Actions that are not selectable should not be displayed. (Do not show disabled state actions)

The search bar, filter chips, and global actions are replaced by the bulk action options.

To exit or escape “batch action mode”, the user can deselect all the items.

When entering into "batch action mode", forbid all inline actions.
When enter into "batch action mode", hide pagination to let users focus on the current selection task.

Support hotkey “command” to do multi-select.

Name: Helper text
Description: Optional, helps the user understand what action they have to perform or can perform.
Dynamic Behavior: N/A

Name: Primary CTA button
Content Rules:
The action word in the CTA should mirror the action in the title. 
e.g  Title: Sign in for access
CTA: Sign in
More on how to write great titles and CTAs for confirmation modals:
https://www.loom.com/share/f8b87cb2213c456baf363a49835bdfa5
Description: Clicking the primary action will complete the task and automatically close the modal dialog box.
Dynamic Behavior: The primary CTA should NEVER be disabled.

Name: Primary button
Description:
Will either have “Done” or “Try it”. Done will Close the modal dialog box.

Try it will take the user to the next flow, closing the modal.
Dynamic Behavior:
The primary CTA should NEVER be disabled. 
We should wait to validate any and all inputs when the CTA is clicked and not before.

Name: Multi-line text input field
Content Rules:
Form labels are typically nouns.

For form labels that require action, start with a verb e.g Select card type.

Form labels should always make it clear which field they should fill in.

Label input fields with short, 1-2 word nouns e.g name, date, area code etc.

Label text should not exceed the width of the input field.

Labels may be truncated but editing for length is preferred.

Avoid overly long labels.

Don’t wrap label text.

Labels don’t need punctuation like periods or colons.

The error message temporarily replaces the field label, so the message must provide instruction on how to fix the error AND include the name of the field to make it clear which field is affected.

State the issue clearly and in a way that does not assign blame.

Provide a remedy when possible.

Provide only the information the user needs to know at this moment.
Default State: The text box is in unselected state, with a form label and helper text on top of the box.
Description: Allows user to enter or edit multi-line string of text for longer, responses. For things like label descriptions, bulk values etc.
Dynamic Behavior: By default, all the text input fields are required. When a text input field is optional, show “(optional)” in placeholder.
Error State: On not adding any value and pressing the CTA results in error message. The label and helper text changes to error message and the text input box’s border changes to red color.

Name: Surface content space
Description: It shows read-only content so the viewer and find and browse relevant content.

Name: Search bar
Default State: The search field would be empty
Description: The search field allows the user to search for specific objects in all the objects in the object list.
Dynamic Behavior:
Entering a search query will refresh the object list with objects that match the search query term. 

The object list will refresh dynamically as the user enters their search query.

The search behavior is based on the current data set (filtering, sorting rule).
Error State: If there are no object results for a search query, the object list should show an empty state graphic.

Name: Number field
Description: Text field that will always be a positive none 0 integer (natural number). The field should automatically validate itself
Dynamic Behavior: If the shortcut selected is anything apart from last quarter or last year this field will not be present.
Error State: This field will need to be validated for any non natural number and will automatically switch to nearest natural number and closest to any decimal entered or should prevent users from entering any decimal or negative values

Name: Filter chip

If the user selects multiple values to filter by, then all the values are listed next to the column name in the chip

Display filter chips in multi-lines when it’s more than one line. Keep 32px space between global button if there is. ../Widgets/Object%20Table%20309374cae3fa8113b439c8bb096e0478.md

The values in the filter chips would be in alphabetical order. If users add a new value, it will added in alphabetical order as well.

Clicking on the filter chip, pop up the dialog box to edit.
Error State: If there are no object results for multi-filters, the object list should show an empty state graphic.

Name: CTA bar
Description: There are three buttons in the Wizard CTA bar: Previous, Cancel, Continue.
Dynamic Behavior:
In the first step, the CTA bar includes Cancel and Continue buttons.

In the middle steps, the CTA bar includes the Previous, Cancel, and Continue buttons.

In the last step, the CTA bar includes Previous, Cancel and complete task buttons.

Name: Primary CTA button
Description: Clicking the primary action will guide users to the next step to complete multiple tasks.
Dynamic Behavior:
The primary CTA would be Continue guiding users to the next step.
In the last step of the sequence, the Continue button label should change to reflect the final action.
The primary CTA should NEVER be disabled.

Name: Cancel/Back button
Description: Clicking the Cancel/Back button will close the current modal and return the user to its previous context. Cancel/Back undoes all applied changes.
Dynamic Behavior:
In the main task dialog box, the CTA is “Cancel”
In the sub-task dialog box, the CTA is “Back”. Clicking the button, go back to the main task dialog box without submitting any data. 
When all the info are submited instantly or just check the info, the primary CTA is “Done”, and there is no need for secondary CTA.

Name: Selection
Description: It’s the item that has been selected.
Dynamic Behavior:
Clicking anywhere in its row can select this object.
Once something is selected, highlight the row with light blue color and a checkmark. 
There must be one object being selected. Clicking on the selected object won’t delect this object.

Description: The tertiary icon , Reset link will reset the conditions configured in all segments to its default value
Dynamic Behavior: Whatever the segment the user is currently in when they click on the reset link it will reset the condition in that segment to its default value

Name: Progress bar

The height of the progress bar is 4 px

Based on the number of steps in wizard , the progress bar will have steppers. The space between the steppers is 4 px.

Name: Column sort/filter headers
Default State: The default state is determined by the needs of the project.
Description:
Column sort headers allow users to reorder an object list.

These are accessed by clicking on the column header.

The headers also has a grey 20 hover state. On hover the cursor changes to a hand to denote click-ability.

https://www.loom.com/share/15ebd8bdb3084863961121ca8f337fd8?sid=aa057ef6-e22d-4bc7-b386-9ba430421402 for hover state.
Dynamic Behavior:
Clicking on the column header will open the menu, users can choose to filter or sort.

Clicking on the Filter will open the filter modal dialog box.

Clicking on the Sort will resort to the object list according to the column type. I.e chronologically, alphabetically, low to high. 

Once clicked the Sort ascending or Sort descending will display an up or down arrow to denote both that this is the column that the object list is sorted by and also the direction of the sort order.

Name: Sort options

Description: The user can select to sort a column with in ascending or descending order.
Dynamic Behavior:
Selecting a sorting option will refresh the object list with the selected sort order. 

A sort direction arrow will be shown next to the column name

Name: Previous button
Description: Go back to previous step
Dynamic Behavior: Save users edit content for them to re-edit or review later

Name: ThoughtSpot logo
Description: Clicking on the logo will return the user to the Insight page.

Name: Single object checkbox
Default State: By default, the check box will be unselected.
Description: This check box allows the user to select a row in the object list and apply an action to it.
Dynamic Behavior:
Clicking the check box selects the row, clicking again will unselect the row. 

Multiple checkboxes can be selected at the same time.

The check box can also be unselected if the user clicks the select all check box at the top left of the object list.

All selection are effected on the current page, if users change to other pages, it won’t count 2 pages selections. 

../Widgets/Object%20Table%20309374cae3fa8113b439c8bb096e0478.md../Widgets/Object%20Table%20309374cae3fa8113b439c8bb096e0478.md

Name: Month selection: Dropdown Input1
Description: This input allows the user to pick the type of monthly frequency they want to set, IF the user has selected the “month” input from the primary frequency dropdown (2)
Dynamic Behavior:
This input allows the user to pick the type of monthly frequency they want to set, IF the user has selected the “month” input from the primary frequency dropdown (2)
Values : “By date”, “On the”

Name: Overlay
Description: #000000 60% opacity
Dynamic Behavior: Clicking outside the passive modal area will automatically close the modal.

Name: Global actions
Default State: In default state, Global action button is in unselected state, it is a secondary button (grey color).
Description: Actions that influence the whole table list, like "Create new", "import" etc.
Dynamic Behavior: Global action could open a dialog box or a window etc. It depends on the use case

Name: Create new
Default State: The text field is in the focus state, so that lets users enter text directly.
Description:
The create new action lets users can add a new object to the list.


https://www.notion.so/309374cae3fa812895d1deffcef72709?pvs=21
Dynamic Behavior: After clicking on the create new button, display input box inline. The cursor shows in the input box directly. Click check mark to complete the task. Click cross button to cancel the task.
Error State: The error message temporarily replaces the field label. Display error icon and error message in red color.

Name: Chevron icon
Default State: Chevron’s arrow is pointing right (closed state).
Description: The chevron icon indicates this row has a deeper level.
Dynamic Behavior: Clicking anywhere in its row will open the nested list. Making the chevron arrow face down.

Name: Label
Content Rules: The title should be brief and clearly describe the dialogs’s task or purpose.
Description: Use the label above the title set the context for the information in the dialog.

Name: Single line input field
Description: Users can enter or edit a string of text.
Dynamic Behavior: By default, all the text input fields are required. When a text input field is optional, show “(optional)” in placeholder.
Error State: The field outline turn to red color

Name: Tertiary CTA button
Description: Place the additional CTA on the far left of the dialog box. It's not the main task action.

Name: Time period field
Description: Dropdown field that will have multiple values to choose the time period
Dynamic Behavior:
Can have any of the below values
1.Second(s)s
2.Min
3.Hour(s)
4.Day
5.Week
6.Month
7.Quarter
8.Year(s)
Error State: NA - It will always have a value

Name: Secondary Column Content types
Description: The secondary columns display a variable amount of meta-information about specific object types in the primary column
Dynamic Behavior:
These columns will have have variable widths. They will evenly space between the Primary column and the action column. 

Use an en dash (---) to represent null or not applicable (N/A) values.

Name: Primary frequency Dropdown
Default State: By default the dropdown will display “Days”.
Description: This dropdown allows the user to select between hour, day, week and month options for overall frequency.
Dynamic Behavior:
If the dropdown selection is “hour” the time dropdown (3) is removed from the widget. 
Values : “Hour”, “Day”, “Week”, “Month”

Description: This allows users can change the column value at once with multi-select rows

Name: Text input combination field
Default State: Empty, unless data is filled then show data.
Description: Allows for longer, multi-line responses, for things like label descriptions.
Dynamic Behavior: By default, all the text input fields are required. When a text input field is optional, show “(optional)” in placeholder.
Error State: The field outline turn to red color

Name: Content area
Description: Contains the information that read only or interact without second submit.
Dynamic Behavior: When the content exceed the maximum allowed vertical space, the scrollbar appears and is limited the content area only.
Error State: We should wait to validate any and all inputs when the CTA is clicked and not before.

Name: Title
Content Rules:
Use the title to communicate the main benefit in an active and personalized way, for example: "Manage your issues", instead of "Issue types."

Try to personalize where you can, for example: "A better experience for your team", instead of "Better team experiences."

Try to limit titles to three words, although four words are permissible if you are using a short article like “an”, “a”, or “the”.
Description: The title provides a concise overview of the contents of the journey.

Name: Checkbox
Default State: By default, this check box will be unselected.

Name: Content area
Description: Contains the information and/or controls needed to complete the modal’s task. It can include message text and components.
Dynamic Behavior: When the content exceed the maximum allowed vertical space, the scrollbar appears and is limited the content area only.
Error State: We should wait to validate any and all inputs when the CTA is clicked and not before.

Name: Header
Content Rules:
Titles are typically nouns
Title should always make it clear which object list below
Title shoud be short with 1-2 word nouns e.g Liveboard, Tab, City etc.
Avoid overly long title
Don’t wrap or truncate title text
Title don’t need punctuation like periods or colons
Title should use sentence-case capitalization
Description: The header title describes the type of the object list content
Dynamic Behavior: Entering a search query will refresh the object list with objects that match the search query term.The object list will refresh dynamically as the user enters their search query

Name: Primary Field
Default State: The natural number 1 will be entered into this field by default.
Description: The primary entry field allows the user to enter an natural number which allows them to set the frequency per hour, day, week or month.
Dynamic Behavior:
The natural number  1 will be entered into this field by default. This is a required field.
Values : 1 to 12? TBD/Depends on product context
Error State: This field will need to be validated for any non natural number and will automatically switch to nearest natural number and closest to any decimal entered or should prevent users from entering any decimal or negative values

Name: CTA bar
Description: Contains the navigational and action buttons. Action buttons, such as Done, Get started, Try it, are on the right.

Name: Cancel button
Description: Clicking the Cancel button will close the modal and return the user to its previous context. Cancel undoes all applied changes.

Name: Content area
Description: Contains the information and/or controls needed to complete the dialogs’s task. It can include message text and components.
Dynamic Behavior: When the content exceed the maximum allowed vertical space, the scrollbar appears and is limited the content area only.
Error State: We should wait to validate any and all inputs when the CTA is clicked and not before.

Name: Label text
Content Rules:
Form labels are typically nouns
For form labels that require action, start with a verb e.g Select card type
Form labels should always make it clear which field they should fill in
Label input fields with short, 1-2 word nouns e.g name, date, area code etc.
 Label text should not exceed the width of the input field. Labels may be truncated but editing for length is preferred
Avoid overly long labels
Don’t wrap label text
Labels don’t need punctuation like periods or colons.
The error message temporarily replaces the field label, so the message must provide instruction on how to fix the error AND include the name of the field to make it clear which field is affected
State the issue clearly and in a way that does not assign blame
Provide a remedy when possible
Provide only the information the user needs to know at this moment
Description: Label text is used to inform users as to what information is requested for a text field.
Dynamic Behavior: When text input isn't accepted, an error message replace the label text until fixed.
Error State: Display error icon and error message in red color
